CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-06-2022 Continued To Mar-01-23 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-19-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Scott Malzahn, Esq. (424.652.7800; smalzahn@waymakelaw.com) and Jared Sohn, Esq. (jsohn@waymakerlaw.com) of Waymaker LLP for Defendant/Moving Party.
No other appearances.

Not argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT PAYWARD, INC., D/B/A KRAKEN'S DEMURRER to COMPLAINT.

Defendant's demurrer to causes of action one and two is sustained with leave to amend.

The following must be met for a claim of conversion:
1. That Plaintiff owned/possessed/had a right to possess the personal property;
2. That Defendant substantially interfered with Plaintiff's property by knowingly or intentionally:
taking possession of the of personal property; or
preventing Plaintiff from having access to the personal property; or
destroying the personal property; or
refusing to return the personal property after Plaintiff demanded its return.
3. That Plaintiff did not consent;
4. That Plaintiff was harmed; and
5. That Defendant's conduct was a substantial factor in causing Plaintiff's harm.

Here, Plaintiff has not pleaded facts sufficient to demonstrate that Defendant knowingly or intentionally took possession of, prevented him from having access to, destroyed, or refused to return his personal property.

Plaintiff also has not pleaded facts sufficient to show there was a violation of Penal Code sec. 496(a) (the conversion claim demurrer is sustained with leave to amend), such that he is entitled to receive treble damages under sec. 496(c).

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/RBU)
